variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I've got thin evidence here—your docs focus on mistakes and barrier repair, not a full beginner protocol. I'll build this with what's in your module plus foundational skincare logic. --- **the core principle:** barrier first, one active at a time, SPF non-negotiable.

you're 22, likely oily/combo at that age. start with a cleanser, moisturizer with ceramides, and SPF.

add one active (retinoid or azelaic) after 2 weeks once the barrier is solid. no exfoliants, no stacking.     **key moves:** don't skip the first 2 weeks. barrier repair is the foundation. if your skin stings or reddens at week 3, pause adapalene and go back to cleanser + moisturizer for another week. once you're stable on adapalene, you can add a second active (like azelaic for acne or vitamin c for brightness), but not before week 8.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 50+
products.description: High-protection broad-spectrum SPF 50 with cosmetic-elegant feel.

products.name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
products.description: OTC retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) — first-line for comedonal acne.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

steps.label: week 1–2
steps.detail: cleanser + moisturizer + SPF only.  let barrier stabilize. no actives yet.

steps.label: week 3–4
steps.detail: introduce adapalene 0.1% 2x/week PM.  watch for irritation. pause if stinging.

steps.label: week 5–6
steps.detail: increase adapalene to 3–4x/week if tolerated.  barrier should feel stronger.

steps.label: week 7–8
steps.detail: move to 5–6x/week if no redness/dryness. assess skin texture and breakouts.

items: pick a gentle cleanser (CeraVe foaming or cetaphil)
get a moisturizer with ceramides + niacinamide (CeraVe PM or cetaphil rich)
buy SPF 30+ (EltaMD UV clear or La Roche-Posay anthelios)
set phone reminders: AM cleanser+moisturizer+SPF, PM cleanser+moisturizer
week 3: add adapalene 2x/week, monitor for irritation
week 5+: increase frequency only if skin feels calm

cards.label: rule for beginners
cards.hint: no stacking acids, retinoids, or vitamin c
